@@ -32,3 +32,69 @@ jobs:
 
       - name: Compile script benchmarks (no run)
         run: cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run
+
+  script-bench-cold-start-observational:
+    name: script-bench-cold-start-observational
+    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
+    timeout-minutes: 30
+    steps:
+      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
+
+      - name: Install Rust toolchain (per rust-toolchain.toml)
+        uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@stable
+        with:
+          # rust-toolchain.toml pins 1.92.0 + components; the action honors it.
+          toolchain: stable
+
+      - name: Cache cargo registry + target
+        uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@v2
+        with:
+          shared-key: script-bench-cold-start-observational
+
+      - name: Run W04 cold-start (3 samples), compute min-of-3, emit summary
+        run: |
+          set -euo pipefail
+          cargo_v="$(cargo --version)"
+          rustc_v="$(rustc --version)"
+          wasmtime_v="$(awk '/^name = "wasmtime"$/{getline; gsub(/"/, "", $3); print $3; exit}' Cargo.lock)"
+          declare -a samples=()
+          for i in 1 2 3; do
+            log="sample_${i}.log"
+            cargo test -p rge-script-bench --release --lib \
+              wasmtime_cranelift::tests::w04_cold_start_wasmtime_cranelift \
+              -- --nocapture > "${log}" 2>&1
+            cat "${log}"
+            ns="$(grep -oE 'duration_ns=[0-9]+' "${log}" | head -1 | cut -d= -f2)"
+            if [ -z "${ns}" ]; then
+              echo "Failed to capture duration_ns from sample ${i}" >&2
+              exit 1
+            fi
+            samples+=("${ns}")
+          done
+          d1="${samples[0]}"
+          d2="${samples[1]}"
+          d3="${samples[2]}"
+          min=$(( d1 < d2 ? d1 : d2 ))
+          min=$(( min < d3 ? min : d3 ))
+          {
+            echo "## W04 raw Wasmtime Cranelift cold-start — observational CI sample"
+            echo ""
+            echo "| Sample | duration_ns |"
+            echo "| ------ | ----------- |"
+            echo "| run 1  | ${d1} |"
+            echo "| run 2  | ${d2} |"
+            echo "| run 3  | ${d3} |"
+            echo "| **min-of-3** | **${min}** |"
+            echo ""
+            echo "| Field | Value |"
+            echo "| ----- | ----- |"
+            echo "| cargo | \`${cargo_v}\` |"
+            echo "| rustc | \`${rustc_v}\` |"
+            echo "| wasmtime (Cargo.lock) | \`${wasmtime_v}\` |"
+            echo ""
+            echo "### Caveats"
+            echo ""
+            echo "- Observational only. No new timing threshold; inherits the harness's \`< 2s\` ceiling assertion."
+            echo "- Runner is GitHub-hosted ubuntu-latest, NOT the DENTAL recorder host."
+            echo "- Numbers are NOT comparable to the \`crates/script-bench/BASELINE.md\` recorder-host row."
+          } >> "$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY"

 > **AI Handoff Protocol**: AI-to-AI dispatch packets (Planner / Executor / Reviewer with human arbitration) follow the append-only governance protocol in [`ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md`](ai_handoffs/AI_HANDOFF_PROTOCOL.md). Templates: [`ai_handoffs/templates/`](ai_handoffs/templates/). The protocol is now also runtime-automated: `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` orchestrates one bounded dispatch (Codex plans / Claude executes / Codex controls, with Claude's `GATE_VERDICT` plan-gate marker and `EXEC_STATUS` / `EXEC_PACKET` execute markers driving control flow), and `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` is the GitHub issue queue runner — it pulls the oldest open `ai-dispatch`-labelled issue, runs it through the loop on a per-issue `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-<n>` branch, writes a timestamped `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` audit file, and auto-publishes only when the loop exits 0 and Codex control says `pass`; failed or blocked runs remain local and are labelled failed. The recurring `/loop` queue cadence is semi-attended and bound to the local authenticated CLI session, not a headless cloud daemon — see [`AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`](AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md). The packets themselves remain a documentation convention, not a replacement for ADRs / `Status.md` / `HANDOFF.md` / `change.md`. Precedent files at the repo root (`OPENAItoCLAUDE_*.md`, `CLAUDEtoOPENAI_*.md`, `CLAUDE_SUB_EPSILON_REVIEW.md`) remain valid historical records and are not migrated.

Wires the existing `crates/script-bench/src/wasmtime_cranelift.rs::tests::w04_cold_start_wasmtime_cranelift` focused release test into GitHub Actions as a no-gate observational signal. **Headline fact**: a new `script-bench-cold-start-observational` job in `.github/workflows/bench.yml` runs the existing focused release test three times on `ubuntu-latest` per `push` to `main` and `pull_request` to `main`, captures each run's `duration_ns` from stdout, computes the min-of-3 in bash arithmetic, and emits a Markdown table to `$GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY` with the three per-run values, the min-of-3, `cargo --version`, `rustc --version`, the `wasmtime` version read from `Cargo.lock`, and a caveat block that explicitly states the job is observational only and inherits the harness's `< 2s` ceiling assertion, that the runner is GitHub-hosted `ubuntu-latest` and NOT the DENTAL recorder host, and that the numbers are NOT comparable to the `crates/script-bench/BASELINE.md` recorder-host row. **No new timing assertion is added**: the job uses default cargo-test failure semantics, inherits the harness's existing `assert!(duration > Duration::ZERO)` + `assert!(duration < Duration::from_secs(2))` invariants unchanged (structurally many orders of magnitude above any plausible CI sample and expected never to trip), and adds no threshold environment variable, no comparison-against-baseline logic, and no helper script. **Local single-sample verification (workspace root, Windows 11 Pro for Workstations recorder host)** for the same command CI invokes: `cargo test -p rge-script-bench --release --lib wasmtime_cranelift::tests::w04_cold_start_wasmtime_cranelift -- --nocapture` produced `w04_cold_start_wasmtime_cranelift: duration_ns=276700 duration_ms=0.277` — used solely to confirm the harness still prints the expected stdout line shape; explicitly **NOT** appended to `crates/script-bench/BASELINE.md` per scope. **What this snapshot supersedes (forward only; older dated snapshots below retained verbatim as history)**: nothing — the prior 2026-05-28 ISSUE-243 snapshot (recorder-host wasmtime 44 rebaseline) stays current; +failure, distinct from slow timing.
